name: Winter Vegetable Stock
recipeIngredient: 4 Outer stalks of celery; plus the celery leaves; diced
1 cup Chard stems; cut into one-inch lengths
1 medium Potato; diced, OR 1/2 cup thick potato scraps
OR Chard leaves, lettuce, or nettles
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the butter or oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Add leek greens, carrots, celery stalks, celery leaves, squash, lentils, thyme sprigs or dried thyme, bay leaves, borage leaves (or chard/lettuce/nettles), sage leaves, parsley stems, and garlic cloves to the pot.
4. Stir in salt and nutritional yeast (if using).
5. Pour in cold water until vegetables are just covered; bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat to low, partially cover the pot, and simmer for 30 to 40 minutes.
7. Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
8. Press down on solids with the back of a wooden spoon to extract as much liquid as possible.
9. Adjust seasoning if needed before using.
Makes 4 to 6 cups.
